rationale: The citing paper explicitly models social-identity moderators (sender/receiver gender groups, vehicle identity relevance, price-tier similarity) in social influence, while the cited paper provides experimental evidence that gender identity congruency with a prior actor changes behavior and specifies boundary conditions (identity esteem, self/other focus). That construct-level overlap suggests the cited work could be a theory/mechanism source rather than a passing mention. With a confirmed DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.45.3.351), this pair should be retained for full relation judgment.
